latest.title: Zegona Holdco Limited TLB reporting as at 31/12/25
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 4295U
Zegona Communications PLC
26 February 2026
Zegona Holdco Limited TLB reporting as at 31/12/25
26 February 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
Publication of Zegona Holdco Limited's debt reporting as at 31 December 2025
·
Zegona is pleased to publish information in relation to Zegona Holdco Limited and its subsidiaries ("Zegona Holdco Group"), for the purpose of satisfying the Zegona Holdco Group's external debt reporting obligations.
·
This includes key performance indicators (KPIs) of Vodafone Spain for the three months ended 31 December 2025 as well as operational commentary summarising the strong Q3 results, which pave the way for a second successful year in Zegona's transformation of Vodafone Spain.
·
The information is set out in the appendix to this announcement.
·
The information can also be found on Zegona's website at
https://www.zegona.com/investor-relations/debt-investors.aspx
For further information, please contact:
For investor enquiries:
For media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns.
Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.
Appendix
Zegona Holdco Limited ("the Company")
A company incorporated in England and Wales, company number 10159604 with registered office address of
8 Sackville Street, London, England, W1S 3DG
Consolidated Financial Information
Unaudited
31 Dec 25
Zegona HoldCo Limited and its subsidiaries ("the Zegona HoldCo Group" or "the Group")
presents the Group's unaudited consolidated financial information for the periods noted
in the following pages.
Purpose of this information
This information is provided for the sole purpose of satisfying the Company's external debt reporting obligations as required pursuant to the Senior Facilities Agreement dated 15 July 2024.
Operational Commentary
Q3 was another strong quarter and the results pave the way for a second successful year in Zegona's transformation of Vodafone Spain. Another quarter of positive customer and product additions, together with the positive impact of the January price initiatives, gives increased confidence the company will close FY26 with revenue stabilisation, in line with prior year.
Continued implementation of business transformation initiatives is also driving profitability and cash flow. Q3 year-to-date cash flow5 of €586m means the business is well placed to close FY26 with cash flow margins over 21%. This would represent a significant improvement from last year's 17% margin and over double the 10% prior to Zegona's acquisition in FY24.
The closing of both FiberCo transactions marks another major milestone in Zegona's delivery of its post-acquisition strategy. The recent EU clearance of the FiberPass investment removes the last hurdle to completing the transformation of Vodafone Spain's fixed network strategy. The combination of FiberPass and PremiumFiber will give guaranteed access to a future-proof, all fibre national network with attractive economic terms.
The combined €1.8b upfront proceeds from the FiberCo transactions create the opportunity to deliver significant returns to our shareholders. The recently announced capital allocation policy involves returning €1.6b of value to shareholders. This included a €1.4b special dividend paid in January 2026 (permitting the settlement in full of the Vodafone Group financing and the 69% reduction in Zegona's ordinary shares) and an ongoing €0.2b share buyback programme. The remaining €0.2b will be used for debt reduction. This puts the business on track to deliver net debt of only €3.2b by the end of our financial year. This, combined with our recent debt refinancing at a cost of debt of only 4.3%, reinforces Zegona's commitment to its leverage target of 1.5x to 2x and further accelerates progress in reducing total annual interest costs.

Notes:
1
Finetwork numbers are excluded in the table above as the acquisition process is still ongoing.
2
Includes both contract and prepay mobile lines. Total Mobile contract lines have increased to 10,155k in the three months to December 2025 from 10,150k as at September 2025.
3
EBITDAaL is defined as earnings attributable to the operating group of companies before income tax credit, net financing costs, amortization of customer-related intangible assets, amortization of owned assets and depreciation of owned assets, excluding gains/losses on disposal of owned and leased assets, restructuring costs, other income and expense and significant items that are not considered by management to be reflective of the underlying performance, including the impacts of depreciation and gain on disposal of leased assets and interest on lease liabilities, and adjusted in line with the parent's accounting policy relating to subscriber acquisition costs.
4
Net debt is shown as calculated for the investor presentations, being the nominal amount of debt (with the SSN USD amounts presented as the hedged EUR value of the nominal amount) and net of cash held by the Zegona Holdco Group as at 31 December 2025 (€647m). Zegona Holdco Group EBITDAaL was €312m for the 3 months to December 2025.
5
Cash flows are defined as EBITDAaL less capital expenditure relating to acquisition of property, plant and equipment, intangible assets and costs relating to customer commercial activity, as well as payments made for dismantling assets and excluding telecommunication licences and financed assets, minus the selling price upon disposal of any assets and changes in asset retirement obligation provisions, as defined in the FY25 Annual Report.

company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona builds and operates fibre‑optic networks that supply high‑speed connectivity to telecom operators and large enterprise customers. The company owns a sizable portfolio of fibre assets and works with partners to expand coverage.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The demand for ultra‑fast data services is rising as cloud, video and remote work become mainstream. Zegona’s fibre network gives operators a cost‑effective way to upgrade capacity without building from scratch, giving the firm a strategic moat in a growing market.
company_30s.current_state: Zegona is in the midst of a major restructuring: it has cut its share count, re‑priced its debt, and entered a new partnership that should unlock additional revenue streams. The company is still paying dividends, which keeps some investors interested, but the market has priced it far below the value of its assets.

rns_narrative: The company has trimmed its share base by 69 % and is tightening governance by adjusting voting rights, signalling a move to protect remaining shareholders and simplify the capital structure.
A debt‑re‑pricing deal completed in January has lowered Zegona’s interest burden, improving cash flow and reducing financial risk.
In February the firm announced a new fibre partnership that should expand its network reach, while a transaction involving its own shares suggests a possible buy‑back or strategic stake management.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around a deep‑discount asset that is getting a financial makeover.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the dividend, the share‑count reduction, and the new fibre partnership as catalysts that could lift the stock once the market digests the changes.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry that the debt restructuring may not fully cover the company’s long‑term obligations and that the share‑count cut could create governance headaches.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has been largely silent, but recent posts about the dividend and the partnership have sparked a modest uptick in bullish chatter.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, giving the setup a high potential for surprise if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The ex‑dividend date in early January should attract dividend‑seeking buyers, giving the stock a temporary lift.
Any announcement that the fibre partnership will generate new revenue streams or that the debt restructuring has fully closed will act as a confidence signal.
A volume spike at the current support level—especially if it exceeds the average daily volume by a wide margin—would confirm that traders are beginning to re‑enter.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to watch over the next two to three months are: (1) a clear statement that the debt deal is final and that cash flow projections are positive; (2) a confirmation that the share‑count reduction has been fully implemented; (3) a surge in trading volume at or just below the current price, indicating a fresh influx of capital.
expect_next.timing_regime: The company appears to be in the early stage of a crash‑recovery cycle: the market has already taken a deep hit, but the structural fixes suggest that a rebound could begin once the market processes the new debt terms and share‑count cut.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Zegona’s deep discount combined with a clean debt balance and a new fibre partnership creates a textbook mean‑reversion scenario. If the stock recovers to the middle of its 52‑week range, the upside is more than double the current price. The historic performance of similar deep‑bottom signals shows that a 70‑95 % crash often leads to a 100‑200 % rebound once the market catches on.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The remaining risk is that the debt deal could fall through, or that the share‑count cut introduces unforeseen governance costs. In that worst‑case scenario the stock could slide further, but it would still stay well above the absolute floor set by the company’s asset base.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: You could lose only the small percentage you invest if the price stays below the current level, but you stand to gain more than double if the mean reversion plays out. The asymmetry is heavily in your favour.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a confirmed volume rebound at the current support level. Use a tight stop just below that support to protect against a false break. Allocate a modest portion of your portfolio—around 5 % of capital—to keep the position manageable while still capturing the upside.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see either (1) the debt‑re‑pricing is officially closed, (2) the share‑count reduction is fully reflected in the filings, or (3) a volume spike at the support level. Once any of those happen, consider a small entry.
playbook.if_passing: There are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy signals. If the company fails to complete the debt restructuring or if regulatory issues arise that could invalidate the share‑count cut, consider stepping back.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Zegona is a heavily discounted fibre asset that has just finished a debt makeover and a share‑count cut; once the market digests those changes, the upside is large and the downside is limited.

panic_analysis.interpretation: 🟠 Fear dominant - watch for reversal signs
panic_analysis.opportunity_reason: 📊 Moderate fear detected - monitor for escalation

fear_vs_facts.verdict: ⚖️ Mixed signals - Fear and facts balanced, wait for clarity
